summary: Initialize search bar auto-completion with real database context (no tab switch needed)

description.text: Closes #11428 This PR ensures that the search bar's auto-completion is correctly initialized as soon as a database is opened, without requiring the user to switch tabs. The auto-completer is now updated immediately after the real database context is loaded, improving the user experience and resolving the issue where auto-completion was unavailable until a tab switch. ### Steps to test 1. **Launch JabRef.** 2. Go to **File → Open library…** and open `jablib/src/main/resources/Chocolate.bib`. 3. Wait until the Chocolate tab finishes loading (spinner disappears). 4. Click the **Global Search** bar (top toolbar) and type `s` or `sm`. **Before** No suggestions appear at this step. **After** Auto-completion popup appears immediately. --- ### Mandatory checks - [x] I own the copyright of the code submitted and I license it under the [MIT license](https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/blob/main/LICENSE) - [x] I manually tested my changes in running JabRef (always required) - [/] I added JUnit tests for changes (if applicable) - [x] I added screenshots in the PR description (if change is visible to the user) - [x] I described the change in `CHANGELOG.md` in a way that is understandable for the average user (if change is visible to the user) - [x] I checked the [user documentation](https://docs.jabref.org/): Is the information available and up to date? If not, I created an issue at or, even better, I submitted a pull request updating file(s) in .

summary: Refine "REDACTED" replacement of API key value in web fetcher search URL

description.text: Closes #13796 Improve URL redaction: change the regex to redact only the API key's value (not the whole name=value). Add tests to cover common key names and edge cases. ### Steps to test In Web search, choose "Springer", search anything, then you will see the error message below: ### Mandatory checks - [x] I own the copyright of the code submitted and I license it under the [MIT license](https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/blob/main/LICENSE) - [x] I manually tested my changes in running JabRef (always required) - [x] I added JUnit tests for changes (if applicable) - [x] I added screenshots in the PR description (if change is visible to the user) - [x] I described the change in `CHANGELOG.md` in a way that is understandable for the average user (if change is visible to the user) - [x] I checked the [user documentation](https://docs.jabref.org/): Is the information available and up to date? summary: Update the search syntax highlight for web search

description.text:
Followup to https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/pull/13691
<!-- LINK THE ISSUE WITH THE "Closes" KEYWORD. Example: Closes https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/issues/13109 OR Closes #13109 -->

Updated the websearch field to be highlighted in accordance with the ANTLR based parser, rather than the old lucene one.

<!-- NOTE: If your work is not yet complete, please open a draft pull request. In that case, outline your intended next steps. Do you need feedback? Will you continue in parallel? ... -->

Writing a query to the web search field should turn it red if the query doesn't obey the search syntax rules.

summary: Automatic field editor: Checkbox: Show only set fields

description.text: Open a library Open automatic field edtior See that at field dropdown ALL available fileds are displayed: When I want to clear fields, I need to know by heart which fields are set. --- Task 1. Add a new tab "Clar content" 2. Make it simlar as existing "Edit content", but only for "clear" 3. Remove "Clear field content" at "Edit conent" If possible, also filter the list at "From" at "Copy or Move content"

Closes #13708
<!-- LINK THE ISSUE WITH THE "Closes" KEYWORD. Example: Closes https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/issues/13709 OR Closes #13109 -->

<!-- In about one to three sentences, describe the changes you have made: what, where, why -->
Fixed a NullPointerException when opening the Print Preview in the Entry Preview tab on macOS ARM. The check for null entries was added to prevent NPE when an entry is not yet initialized.

<!-- NOTE: If your work is not yet complete, please open a draft pull request. In that case, outline your intended next steps. -->
<!-- This PR is ready for review. No additional steps are planned. -->

### Steps to test

1. Open JabRef on macOS (ARM) or any OS.  
2. Create a new entry.  
3. Open the **Preview** tab.  
4. Right-click → **Print Preview**.  
5. Verify that no exception occurs and the preview renders correctly.  

<!-- You can add screenshots or videos if needed. -->

summary: Systematic Literature Research gives 'study repository not found' error

description.text:
### JabRef version Latest development branch build (please note build date below) ### Operating system GNU / Linux ### Details on version and operating system Ubuntu 25.04 ### Checked with the latest development build (copy version output from About dialog) - [x] I made a backup of my libraries before testing the latest development version. - [x] I have tested the latest development version and the problem persists ### Steps to reproduce the behaviour 1- Build and run the up-to-date repository 2-Select tools -> start new systematic literature review 3-Fill study title, author, questions and query fields, select a catalog 4-Select empty directory and start survey The action will fail and the user will get an error message saying the repository couldn't be created. ### Appendix This issue is present at the time of writing which is 27.09.2025 ...
Screenshot of the error
